name: Validation/validate-modes
description: $validate operation conformance tests ported from Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E.Rest.ValidateTests (fhir-server). Covers scenarios not already exercised by Validation/validate-op: validating against a specific 'profile' parameter (both as a query string and as an embedded Parameters 'profile' parameter) for conformant and non-conformant resources, a resourceType mismatch between the request URL and the resource body, a required-element (cardinality) violation on a non-Patient resource type, instance-level $validate via GET for an existing resource id (conformant and non-conformant) and for a missing id (404), and an unresolvable/malformed 'profile' parameter value. Assertions inspect the OperationOutcome for error/fatal issues rather than exact diagnostic text, and profile-enforcement assertions are warningOnly because custom StructureDefinition-based profile validation support varies across FHIR servers.

variable.name: profileSdId
variable.description: The server-assigned id of the custom validation profile StructureDefinition created for this suite

variable.name: conformantPatientId
variable.description: The server-assigned id of the Patient that conforms to the custom profile (has birthDate)

variable.name: nonconformantPatientId
variable.description: The server-assigned id of the Patient that does not conform to the custom profile (missing birthDate)

operation.description: Create a custom StructureDefinition profile that constrains Patient.birthDate to 1..1, used by the profile-parameter tests below
Create a Patient with birthDate set, so instance-level (GET by id) $validate tests have a profile-conformant resource to reference
Create a Patient without birthDate, so instance-level (GET by id) $validate tests have a profile-non-conformant resource to reference

test.name: type-level $validate: profile-conformant resource accepted (profile via query parameter)
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate?profile=<url> with a raw (non-Parameters-wrapped) Patient that has birthDate set must return an OperationOutcome with no error/fatal issues against the custom profile requiring birthDate.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate?profile=... with a conformant Patient (raw body)

assert.description: Operation should return a 2xx success status (warningOnly: some servers use 422 for validation failures)
Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Conformant resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: type-level $validate: profile-conformant resource accepted (profile via Parameters parameter)
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate with a Parameters-wrapped body containing both a 'profile' parameter and a conformant Patient 'resource' parameter must return an OperationOutcome with no error/fatal issues.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate with profile + conformant resource both embedded in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Conformant resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: type-level $validate: profile-non-conformant resource rejected (profile via query parameter)
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate?profile=<url> with a raw Patient missing birthDate must be flagged against the custom profile requiring birthDate (warningOnly: enforcement of custom uploaded profiles varies across FHIR servers).

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate?profile=... with a non-conformant Patient (raw body, no birthDate)

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Non-conformant resource: OperationOutcome should contain an error or fatal issue (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers)

test.name: type-level $validate: profile-non-conformant resource rejected (profile via Parameters parameter)
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate with a Parameters-wrapped body containing a 'profile' parameter and a non-conformant Patient 'resource' parameter should be flagged (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers).

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate with profile + non-conformant resource both embedded in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Non-conformant resource: OperationOutcome should contain an error or fatal issue (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers)

test.name: type-level $validate: resourceType in the URL must match resourceType in the body
test.description: POST /Observation/$validate with a Parameters-wrapped Patient resource (resourceType mismatch between URL and body) must return an OperationOutcome reporting at least one error/fatal issue. Ported from GivenAValidateRequest_WhenAnInvalidResourceIsPassedByParameter_ThenADetailedErrorIsReturned.

operation.description: POST to Observation/$validate with a Patient resource embedded in Parameters

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Type mismatch: OperationOutcome must contain at least one error or fatal issue
An issue should reference the type mismatch via a 'TypeName' expression (warningOnly: exact expression/message format varies across servers)

test.name: type-level $validate: required element missing on a non-Patient resource (Observation.status)
test.description: POST /Observation/$validate with an Observation missing the required 'status' element (cardinality 1..1) must return an OperationOutcome reporting at least one error/fatal issue. Ported from GivenAValidateRequest_WhenTheResourceIsInvalid_ThenADetailedErrorWithLocationsIsReturned.

operation.description: POST to Observation/$validate with an Observation that omits the required status element

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Missing required element: OperationOutcome must contain at least one error or fatal issue
An issue should reference Observation.status (warningOnly: issue expression format varies across servers)

test.name: instance-level $validate (GET by id): conformant resource passes
test.description: GET /Patient/{id}/$validate?profile=<url> for a stored Patient that conforms to the custom profile must return an OperationOutcome with no error/fatal issues. Ported from GivenAValidateByIdRequest_WhenTheResourceIsValid_ThenAnOkMessageIsReturned.

operation.description: GET Patient/{id}/$validate?profile=... for the profile-conformant stored Patient

assert.description: Operation should return a 2xx success status (warningOnly: some servers use 422 for validation failures)
Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Conformant resource: OperationOutcome must have no error or fatal issues

test.name: instance-level $validate (GET by id): non-conformant resource is rejected
test.description: GET /Patient/{id}/$validate?profile=<url> for a stored Patient that does not conform to the custom profile should be flagged (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers). Ported from GivenAValidateByIdRequestWithStricterProfile_WhenRunningValidate_ThenAnErrorShouldBeReturned.

operation.description: GET Patient/{id}/$validate?profile=... for the profile-non-conformant stored Patient

assert.description: Response body must be an OperationOutcome
Non-conformant resource: OperationOutcome should contain an error or fatal issue (warningOnly: custom profile enforcement varies across servers)

test.name: instance-level $validate (GET by id): non-existent id returns 404
test.description: GET /Patient/{id}/$validate for an id that does not exist on the server must return 404 Not Found. Ported from GivenUnpresentIdRequest_WhenValidateIt_ThenAnErrorShouldBeReturned.

operation.description: GET Patient/{missing-id}/$validate for an id that was never created

assert.description: Server must return 404 Not Found for a non-existent id

test.name: type-level $validate: malformed profile parameter value
test.description: POST /Patient/$validate?profile=<not-a-resolvable-url> with an otherwise-valid Patient. Ported from GivenInvalidProfile_WhenValidateCalled_ThenBadRequestReturned. Marked warningOnly because some servers reject unresolvable profile references with 400 Bad Request while others silently ignore them and validate against the base type.

operation.description: POST to Patient/$validate?profile=not-a-resolvable-profile-url with a valid Patient body

assert.description: Server should return 400 Bad Request for an unresolvable profile reference (warningOnly: some servers ignore unknown profiles instead)

operation.description: Teardown: delete the custom validation profile StructureDefinition
